issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. Text intended for screen readers will be visible on screen, disrupting the layout.

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only defines outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset without an outline width produces no visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users unable to see which element is focused. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for button elements. This removes the focus ring that keyboard users rely on to track their current position.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. A correct sr-only pattern requires absolute positioning and 1px dimensions with clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who have requested reduced motion, which may trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum for touch targets. While this meets the absolute minimum of 24px, users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button without accidentally hitting adjacent elements.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector only adjusts outline-offset to -3px but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. Without an explicit outline width and style, no focus ring is rendered, making it invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector only changes outline-color but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. Setting a color without an outline width produces no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended minimum for touch targets. At 28px, this button is smaller than a fingertip and may be difficult to activate accurately.

issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. This makes the checkbox difficult or impossible to activate for some users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size on footer navigation links

This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not create a visible indicator.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1318: `.pricing-card h3 { font-size: var(--font-size-2xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-tight); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .price-amount
issuesFound.explanation: The price amount uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which can make monetary values difficult to read accurately for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: `.price-amount { font-size: var(--font-size-5xl); font-weight: var(--font-weight-black); color: var(--colour-neutral-900); letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); line-height: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff). This creates a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1) and large text (3:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 993: `.section-header p { font-size: var(--font-size-lg); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #ffffff ratio 2.6:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a white background, resulting in approximately 2.6:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (needs 4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 884: `.logo-bar-label { text-align: center; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-widest); margin-bottom: var(--space-8); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius, providing no visible focus indicator. This removes focus indication for all interactive elements that rely on the default browser focus style, making keyboard navigation impossible for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a replacement. Since box-shadow is suppressed in forced-colors mode, users with high contrast themes will have no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline with no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button removes the focus outline without providing a visible replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline without replacement. This prevents keyboard users from seeing which menu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without replacement, making focus invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons have a minimum width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have a fixed width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, the small size may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible dimensions instead of position: absolute and 1px sizing. This makes the content visible to sighted users and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Search input focus-visible lacks visible indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule removes the outline entirely (outline: none) without providing a box-shadow or border replacement specific to :focus-visible. While the :focus state has a box-shadow, that declaration is overridden for keyboard focus, leaving no visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Button component focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus ring. As a primary interactive component used throughout the interface, this prevents keyboard users from identifying which button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule suppresses the outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change. This mobile menu button becomes invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for the search submit button when accessed via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement, leaving no visual indication when account navigation links receive keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none, which removes the focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode where box-shadow is not supported. This leaves users with no visible focus indication in high-contrast environments.

issuesFound.title: Small button variant touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. This creates a touch target that is too small for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are frequently used in dense toolbars where small targets are particularly problematic.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target potentially below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target. While above the 24px minimum, it falls in the 24-43px range without sufficient padding compensation to reach the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links touch target potentially below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav a selector sets min-height: 24px exactly, placing it in the 24-43px range without padding compensation to achieve the 44px recommended touch target size for users with coarse pointing devices.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Form checkboxes are frequently clicked targets that must be accessible to users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which renders the content visible in the layout instead of visually hiding it while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero primary button missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector declares a transition including transform, which animates spatial movement on hover/focus. No prefers-reduced-motion media query overrides this specific selector, potentially triggering motion sickness.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform var(--transition-slower), animating the 3D perspective transform on hover. Users who prefer reduced motion have no mechanism to disable this spatial animation.

issuesFound.title: Feature card transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-base)), causing the card to lift on hover. This spatial animation lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link arrow animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after pseudo-element has transition: transform var(--transition-fast), which animates the arrow movement on hover. This spatial transition is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Stat item transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item includes transform in its transition (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)), causing lift animation on hover without a mechanism to disable it for users with motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transform animation missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-base)), creating a lift effect on hover that cannot be disabled by users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover transform missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), which is a spatial animation not covered by the existing .pricing-card:hover reduced-motion override (which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text horizontally and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word-spacing can cause words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links without ensuring non-colour visual differentiators (such as font-weight, border-bottom, or background highlights) are present for all link instances. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring. This removes keyboard focus indication for all elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the browser's default focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.


issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa, which on a white background (#ffffff) provides a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for large text (which requires 3:1) and is used for text elements like .section-header p (18px) and .hero-lead (20px).
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~25: 
```css
--colour-text-secondary:   #767676;
```
And verify usage maintains contrast.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible pseudo-class
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Since outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline, keyboard users see no focus indicator on any interactive element unless overridden by more specific rules.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets min-height: 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for AAA compliance, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline. The negative offset alone cannot create a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle sets min-height and min-width to 28px, below the recommended 44px target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target requirement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards include transform in their transition (transition: ... transform var(--transition-base)) and have a hover transform (transform: translateY(-3px)). No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for these spatial animations.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color without a visible outline width. No focus ring appears because the outline width remains zero.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can significantly impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making text harder to parse.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some specific link types have hover underlines or other indicators, the base style relies on color alone to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 329: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only uses outline-offset, which is invisible without a visible outline. This removes the focus ring for all buttons using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 483: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button sets outline: none without a visible replacement, hiding focus from keyboard users trying to open the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links set outline: none without a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which menu item is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input :focus-visible state overrides the :focus box-shadow with outline: none and no replacement, removing the focus ring specifically for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 670: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none without a visible alternative, hiding focus from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 686: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indicators for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none without a visible alternative, making focus invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 898: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set outline: none without a visible replacement, hiding focus from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper sets outline: none without a visible replacement, hiding focus from keyboard users trying to scroll the table.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1223: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none without a visible alternative, removing focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1363: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set outline: none without a visible alternative, hiding focus from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1432: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links set outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1453: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none without a visible alternative, hiding focus from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1462: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1472: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has a min-height and min-width of 28px, which is below the minimum 44px recommended for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8).

issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons have a min-height of 28px, which is below the 44px minimum recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px minimum recommended size for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 626: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a height of 28px, which is below the 44px minimum recommended size for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: #search-input { height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a width and height of 28px, below the 44px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 679: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .product-body .btn-secondary 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: .product-body .btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1123: .pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have a width and height of 20px, significantly below the 44px minimum recommended size for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1431: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, below the 44px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1461: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have a min-height of 28px, below the 44px minimum recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1471: .social-links a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. It effectively does nothing to hide content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the global prefers-reduced-motion block is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform transitions for hover effects. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 778) is empty and does not disable the transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change colour on hover, relying on colour alone to indicate interactivity which fails WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1462: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }

errorMessage: Ollama HTTP 500: {"StatusCode":500,"Status":"500 Internal Server Error","error":"Internal Server Error (ref: 8fa3040f-e007-444f-b6cb-a375ff79dd7b)"}

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine the invalid state of the input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3502: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed when a submenu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1336 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 1358 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function sets it to "false", the open function omits this, leaving screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1332: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `if (inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: characterCounter missing aria-describedby association
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter element is inserted into the DOM but is not programmatically associated with the input field via aria-describedby. Screen readers will not announce the character count when the field is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3830 (after creating counterEl): Add `counterEl.id = srUtils.ensureId(counterEl, 'char-counter');` and `setAttr(field, 'aria-describedby', counterEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing tab roles
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons use aria-selected (implying a tab pattern) but lack the required role="tab" on the buttons and role="tablist" on the container. Screen readers cannot identify this as a tab interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490 (after finding periodContainer): Add `periodContainer.setAttribute('role', 'tablist');`. Line 2495 (inside _syncToggleState or when iterating buttons): Add `btn.setAttribute('role', 'tab');` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user hovers over or clicks a column header to highlight a plan, the visual change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed which plan column is currently emphasized.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2945: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.planHighlighted || '{plan} plan highlighted').replace('{plan}', capitalise(planId)));` inside the highlightColumn function after toggling the CSS class.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a focus trap variable but never instantiates or activates it. When the drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115 (in init): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after `_initialised = true;`. Line 1168 (in open): Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the menu is expanded, and the button state remains incorrect.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function sets aria-expanded="false" but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu closes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not set aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function removes the open class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. If CSS does not use display:none for the hidden state, the content remains accessible to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The button state remains false while the menu is visually open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open and close functions do not announce state changes
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visibility but do not write to a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when dropdowns open or close.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The button state remains false while the answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function sets aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the collapse action to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed when an answer collapses.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies will not identify the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function displays the consent dialog but does not announce its appearance to the live region. Screen reader users may not be aware the dialog has opened.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play function updates aria-pressed but does not announce "Auto-play started" to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the mode change.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel pause function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.pause function updates aria-pressed but does not announce "Auto-play paused" to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the mode change.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher set function announcement uses variable that may be undefined
issuesFound.explanation: The themeSwitcher.set function announces the theme change but constructs the label object inside the function. If STRINGS.themeLight etc. are missing, it falls back to pref which is safe, but the concatenation logic is slightly fragile.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState uses aria-selected on non-tablist buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-selected on period buttons. If these buttons are not inside a role="tablist", aria-selected is invalid. aria-pressed should be used for standalone toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters announces count but not filter name
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function announces "{n} products shown" but does not specify which filter was applied. Screen reader users may not know why the count changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1953:       liveRegion.announce(
  Line 1954:         totalMatches === 0
  Line 1955:           ? (STRINGS.noProductsFound || 'No products found for this filter.')
  Line 1956:           : (STRINGS.productsFiltered || '{n} product{s} shown for {filter}')
  Line 1957:               .replace('{n}', String(totalMatches))
  Line 1958:               .replace('{s}', totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : '')
  Line 1959:               .replace('{filter}', s.activeCategory)

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _onInput does not announce search start
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._onInput function triggers a fetch but does not announce "Searching..." immediately. Users may not know input was registered until results appear.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~850: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true". The _closeInstance function sets it to "false", so screen readers only hear the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1400: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1275: Before the closing brace, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". The close() function sets it to "false", leaving screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2550: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the visual change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that a menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1405: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1420: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at the end of the function (before emit).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not set aria-hidden="true" on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer closes, if the CSS uses transform or off-screen positioning rather than display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden="true", allowing screen reader navigation into a visually hidden menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1260: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside the close function after removing the open class.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not remove aria-hidden from drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile nav drawer opens, if it was previously hidden with aria-hidden="true", the attribute is not removed, keeping it hidden from screen readers despite being visually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1240: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` inside the open function after adding the open class.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). Keyboard focus is not trapped within the open drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1220: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() missing aria-haspopup on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu trigger button does not have aria-haspopup="true" or "menu", failing to indicate to screen readers that it controls a popup menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1225: After ensuring aria-expanded, add `if (!_trigger.getAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item collapses, no announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the answer has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2580: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts cookies, the banner hides without announcing the action result. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that their preference was saved.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3350: After `_store(ACCEPTED);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesAccepted || 'Cookie preferences saved: Analytics enabled');`.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user declines non-essential cookies, the banner hides without announcing the action. Users are not informed that only essential cookies will be used.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3360: After `_store(DECLINED);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookiesDeclined || 'Cookie preferences saved: Only essential cookies');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The open function adds classes and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers with incorrect state information (false) when the menu is actually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1670: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers with incorrect state information when the dropdown is actually open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the panel and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers with incorrect state information when the answer is actually expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2770: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function opens the dropdown but does not announce the action to screen readers via a live region. Users may not be aware the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function closes the dropdown but does not announce the action to screen readers via a live region. Users may not be aware the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1875: liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded function does not announce collapse action via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function announces when the table expands but fails to announce when it collapses. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded when opened programmatically or via toggle, as only the close function resets the state to false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function updates the UI and aria-expanded state but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region. The open function announces the state change, creating an inconsistent experience for screen reader users who miss the close confirmation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function correctly sets it to false, leaving the ARIA state out of sync when the dropdown is opened.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function changes visible content but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens, violating WCAG 4.1.3 for status messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1525:     emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });
  Line 1526:     liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu closes, violating WCAG 4.1.3 for status messages.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1543:     emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });
  Line 1544:     liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question button. The close function correctly sets it to false, causing the ARIA state to incorrectly report the item as collapsed when it is actually open.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel but does not announce "answer collapsed" to the live region. The open function announces the expansion, so the lack of a collapse announcement creates an inconsistent screen reader experience.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to identify invalid fields, so this state change is not communicated without the attribute.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play function starts auto-rotation and updates aria-pressed but does not announce "Auto-play started" to the live region. Users are not informed that the carousel state has changed to automatic rotation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3258:     _scheduleNext();
  Line 3259:     liveRegion.announce('Auto-play started');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel pause function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.pause function stops auto-rotation and updates aria-pressed but does not announce "Auto-play paused" to the live region. Users are not informed that the carousel state has changed to manual control.

fpTitles: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to live region

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, failing to establish the programmatic relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though the close function correctly sets it to "false"). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in function open: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never updated to reflect whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _closeInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line after updating prices: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the result count and filter changes are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterAll || 'No products found' : STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden using CSS classes that likely position it off-screen or change visibility, but aria-hidden="true" is not set when closing. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when not visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in function close: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line in function open: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared to hold a focus trap instance, but it is never assigned or activated when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page content.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without announcing the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies are not informed when content is shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in function open: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) : `${item.title} answer expanded`);`
  Line in function close: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) : `${item.title} answer collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus is moved, but the action is not announced via a live region, leaving screen reader users uncertain what occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies may not realize a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`
  Line in _closeInstance: `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (`_trigger`) is not assigned `aria-expanded="false"` during initialization. Screen readers will not know the menu is closed by default, causing confusion about the current state before the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions toggle the navigation visibility but fail to update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Assistive technology users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, no message is sent to the ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not notified that the menu has closed, especially when closing via outside click or breakpoint change.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open and close functions modify the panel visibility but do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu does not trigger an ARIA live region announcement. Users are not informed of the state change when interacting with navigation sub-menus.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function sets the panel visibility and CSS classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed which answer is currently visible or hidden.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox but `aria-expanded` is not initialized to "false" when the listbox is mounted. It is only set during open/close interactions.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` utility links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` utility removes the error description but does not remove or reset `aria-invalid`. The field may remain announced as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: Applying product filters updates the grid and count badge but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the new filter.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal does not announce changes
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter chip removes the filter but does not announce the action. Screen reader users are not notified that the filter has been cleared.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices but does not announce the change. Users are not informed they are now viewing monthly or annual pricing.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause does not announce state
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling auto-play on the carousel does not announce the state change. Users are not informed whether the carousel is currently rotating or paused.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent hide does not announce dismissal
issuesFound.explanation: Hiding the cookie consent banner does not announce the dismissal. Users are not confirmed that their preference has been saved and the banner closed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4724: In markInvalid function, add `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap and does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay. Additionally, when the drawer closes, focus is not returned to the hamburger button that opened it, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (focus order) and 2.4.7 (focus visible).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2462: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line 2387: In the open function, add `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`
  Line 2410: In the close function, ensure `_trap.deactivate();` is called (currently guarded by `if (_trap && _trap.isActive())` but _trap is never initialized)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers have aria-expanded initialized to false, but the attribute is never updated to true when the panel opens or back to false when it closes. Assistive technologies cannot announce the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2553: In _openInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 2566: In _closeInstance, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to true (the close function sets it to false, but open omits setting it to true). This leaves screen readers unaware that content is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4344: In the open function, add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count and category changes are visible but not announced to screen reader users via an aria-live region. Users are not informed that filtering has occurred or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3716: In applyFilters function, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the visible prices but does not announce the change to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that prices have changed on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4127: In setPeriod function, after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not removed or set to false, potentially leaving stale error state information on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4738: In markValid function, add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-controls set to reference the drawer element's ID on page load, preventing screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2462: In init function, after ensuring IDs exist, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce open/close state via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes the visible content but does not provide an auditory announcement via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize the menu has appeared or disappeared unless they manually check.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2553: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 2566: In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion does not announce expand/collapse via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the content visibility but is not announced to screen reader users. Since focus remains on the trigger button (not moved to the new content), a live region announcement is needed to confirm the action.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4344: In open function, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);`
  Line 4370: In close function, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1598: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1613: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` sets `aria-expanded="false"` in the `close` function but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` when opening an item. This leaves the state inconsistent for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2368: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` helper function adds `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Form validation errors are not programmatically exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2088: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` helper function removes `aria-describedby` but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"` on the field. Invalid state may persist in the accessibility tree after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2103: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function in `productFilter` toggles the CSS `is-active` class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. The selected state is not available to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2735: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function updates the text content of the product count element (`[data-product-count]`) but does not announce the change via a live region. Users are not informed when the number of products changes.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateSavingsMessage` function in the `pricing` module updates the text of the savings message element (`[data-savings-message]`) without a live region announcement. Changes in billing period savings are not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount` function initializes the search combobox but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the input element. The closed state is not explicitly defined on load.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onAccept` and `_onDecline` functions in `cookieConsent` close the banner and update analytics but do not announce the consent decision to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their choice was registered.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The `_animate` function in `statCounterAnimator` dynamically updates the text content of stat elements (`[data-stat-number]`) without using a live region or `aria-live`. The counting animation is silent to assistive technology.

Line 3378: el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line 3365: addClass(el, VISIBLE_CLS); el.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _renderResult, inside `if (!result.valid)` block, after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line (in srUtils.markInvalid function, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line (in _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields

Line (in _renderResult, inside `if (result.valid)` block, after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.fix:
Line 3035:   emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });
  Line 3036:   liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The invalid state may persist in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions do not announce the answer expansion or collapse to screen readers via the liveRegion module, unlike the search filter function which does.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function sets up the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button. The initial state is not exposed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions emit custom events but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Navigation menu opened") to screen readers via the liveRegion module.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. The expanded state is not reflected in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated on close
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. The collapsed state is not reflected in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-controls and aria-haspopup but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The initial closed state is not exposed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions do not announce the dropdown state change to screen readers via the liveRegion module.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger aria-expanded not updated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge not a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the count badge, but the element ([data-product-count]) is not configured as a live region (missing aria-live). Changes are not announced.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action to screen readers. The keyboard shortcut version announces, but the button click does not.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1915:     setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300);
  Line 1916:     liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent decision not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept and _onDecline functions hide the banner and update analytics state but do not announce the consent decision to screen readers via the liveRegion module.
