issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons indicate selection state via the `value` prop but do not use `aria-pressed` to communicate which billing period is currently selected to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute on the underlying `<input>` element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users when form validation fails.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div has interactive onClick without button role
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay `<div>` has an `onClick` handler to close the modal but lacks `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and keyboard event handlers. This makes the click-to-close functionality inaccessible to keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1850-1851: Remove onClick from overlay div or add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler:
    <div className="modal-overlay" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter buttons toggle an active state (activeFilter) visually via className but lack aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot perceive which category is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1106: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: This button acts as a toggle for billing period selection but lacks aria-pressed. The selected state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1153: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle Annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: This button acts as a toggle for billing period selection but lacks aria-pressed. The selected state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm Email input has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the FormGroup component does not receive an errorId prop, so the error message element with that ID is never rendered. Screen readers announce a non-existent ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1417: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar track div is not focusable despite onFocus handler
issuesFound.explanation: The div uses onFocus to pause the marquee, but div elements are not focusable by default. Keyboard users cannot focus this element to pause the animation, violating WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide).
issuesFound.fix: Line 999: <div tabIndex={0} role="region" aria-label="Integration logos" className={['integrations-bar__track', (reducedMotion || isPaused) ? 'integrations-bar__track--paused' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onMouseEnter={() => setIsPaused(true)} onMouseLeave={() => setIsPaused(false)} onFocus={() => setIsPaused(true)} onBlur={() => setIsPaused(false)}>

fpTitles: IntegrationsBar track div is not focusable despite onFocus handler

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure button that toggles the navigation submenu has aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot announce whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1039: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.
  Line 1039: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as a toggle group where one button is always active, but they lack aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is currently selected, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1680: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element.
  Line 1680: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when an error exists, but the FormGroup component is not passed an errorId prop, so the element with that ID is never rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2309: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error} to FormGroup.
  Line 2309: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure trigger button in NavDropdown controls the visibility of a sub-menu via the isOpen prop, but does not communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle} >

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in FaqItemComponent controls the visibility of the answer panel via the isOpen prop, but does not communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen reader users will not know if the answer is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1393: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)} >

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but fails to apply the aria-invalid attribute to the underlying <input> element. When the form field is in an error state, assistive technology will not announce it as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 727: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing keyboard support

issuesFound.explanation: The section expand button has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot perceive whether the sub-menu is open or closed, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: MobileNav component, button element: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button props. <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the asterisk character separately from the "(required)" text that follows in the VisuallyHidden span, causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: FormGroup component, span element: Add aria-hidden="true". *

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce these characters, which is redundant since the aria-expanded state already conveys the information.
issuesFound.fix: NavDropdown component, span element: Add aria-hidden="true". {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component contains a nav element without an aria-label. While it is the first navigation landmark, it should still have a descriptive label to distinguish it from other navigation regions on the page.
issuesFound.fix: SkipLinks component, nav element: Add aria-label="Skip navigation". <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">...</nav>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop per its TypeScript interface, but ThemeToggle renders it without one. Icon-only buttons without accessible names are unreachable to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: ThemeToggle component, IconButton element: Add aria-label prop. <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection region missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection section element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute despite having a visible heading with id="hero-heading". Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify the purpose of this region.
issuesFound.fix: HeroSection component, section element: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading". <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">...</section>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but the close button in MobileNav is rendered without one. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of this icon-only button.
issuesFound.fix: MobileNav component, IconButton element: Add aria-label="Close navigation menu". <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton requires an aria-label prop but is rendered without one. The previous slide button contains only the "‹" character, which is not sufficient for screen reader users to understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: TestimonialsCarousel component, IconButton element: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial". <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton requires an aria-label prop but is rendered without one. The next slide button contains only the "›" character, which is not sufficient for screen reader users to understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: TestimonialsCarousel component, IconButton element: Add aria-label="Next testimonial". <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an `invalid` prop but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: TextInput component, input element: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}. <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component receives an `invalid` prop but does not map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Textarea component, textarea element: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}. <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an `htmlFor` prop but does not apply it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and the input field, preventing screen reader users from identifying the purpose of the form control.
issuesFound.fix: FormGroup component, label element: Add htmlFor={htmlFor} attribute.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" but the ErrorMessage component is only rendered when FormGroup receives an errorId prop, which is not passed. This creates a dangling ID reference that points to a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: NewsletterForm component, FormGroup element: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} props. <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-name-error" but the ErrorMessage is conditionally rendered by FormGroup only when errorId is provided, which is not passed. This creates an invalid ID reference.
issuesFound.fix: ContactForm component, FormGroup element for name field: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" and errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} props. <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-email-error" but the ErrorMessage is not rendered because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop. This creates an invalid ID reference.
issuesFound.fix: ContactForm component, FormGroup element for email field: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} props. <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" but the ErrorMessage is not rendered because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop. This creates an invalid ID reference.
issuesFound.fix: ContactForm component, FormGroup element for subject field: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" and errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} props. <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references aria-describedby including "contact-message-error" but the ErrorMessage is not rendered because FormGroup lacks the errorId prop. This creates an invalid ID reference.
issuesFound.fix: ContactForm component, FormGroup element for message field: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" and errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} props. <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (footer, social, etc.), this landmark should have a unique accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: DesktopNav component, nav element: Add aria-label="Main navigation". <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">...</nav>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section element lacks an aria-labelledby attribute despite having a heading with id="stats-heading". This prevents screen reader users from identifying the statistics region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: StatsBar component, section element: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading". <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">...</section>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading prop but does not set aria-busy="true" when loading. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is busy processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Button component, button element: Add aria-busy={isLoading ? "true" : undefined}. <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? "true" : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button component loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The decorative loading spinner span inside the Button component is not marked with aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may attempt to describe the spinner element, which is redundant since the button already provides a loading label via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Button component, span element: Add aria-hidden="true". <span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The second unordered list in the IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for seamless looping but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the integration partners twice, creating a repetitive and confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix:
IntegrationsBar component, second ul element: Add aria-hidden="true".

issuesFound.explanation: The trust badges container is a group of related content that lacks an accessible name. Screen reader users navigating by regions cannot identify the purpose of this group.
issuesFound.fix:
HeroSection component, div element: Add aria-label="Trust badges".

issuesFound.title: StarRating container missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component uses aria-label on a span but lacks role="img". Without the image role, screen readers may not treat the aria-label as the accessible name for the rating widget.
issuesFound.fix: StarRating component, outer span element: Add role="img". ...

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters are not marked with aria-hidden="true". Since the parent element already provides an accessible name for the entire rating, the individual star characters create redundant noise for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: StarRating component, inner span elements: Add aria-hidden="true". {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: Skip navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element wrapping the skip links is one of multiple navigation landmarks on the page (alongside header, mobile, and footer navs). Without an aria-label, screen reader users cannot distinguish this "Skip Links" navigation from the others when jumping between landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Navigation dropdown trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> element in NavDropdown controls the visibility of a submenu (via isOpen state), but lacks aria-expanded. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed when focusing the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Decorative chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The <span> containing the ▲/▼ chevron characters in NavDropdown is purely decorative context for the button text. Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce the arrow characters redundantly or confusingly.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav id="main-nav"> element is one of multiple navigation landmarks on the page. It lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other nav regions (e.g., footer, mobile, skip links) for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 565: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element inside MobileNav (className="mobile-nav__body") is a distinct navigation region. It lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other nav landmarks for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: <nav aria-label="Mobile" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: Mobile menu toggle button missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> in SiteHeader toggles the mobile navigation panel (isOpen state). While aria-label changes, aria-expanded is the standard attribute to convey the open/closed state of a controlled menu to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle icon button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton in ThemeToggle contains only an emoji icon (☀️/🌙/💻) with no visible text. It is missing the required aria-label prop defined in the IconButton interface, leaving screen reader users without an accessible name for the button.

issuesFound.title: Video play/pause toggle missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button in HeroSection toggles the video playing state (isPlaying). It functions as a two-state toggle but lacks aria-pressed to communicate whether the video is currently playing or paused to assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: Statistics section missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <section> element in StatsBar contains a visually hidden heading (sr-only), but the section itself does not reference it via aria-labelledby. Screen readers navigating by landmarks will encounter an unnamed section region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Monthly billing toggle button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The "Monthly" <button> in BillingToggle acts as a radio-like toggle for billing period selection. It lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether it is the currently selected option.

issuesFound.title: Annual billing toggle button missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The "Annual" <button> in BillingToggle acts as a radio-like toggle for billing period selection. It lacks aria-pressed to indicate whether it is the currently selected option.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> in FaqItemComponent toggles the visibility of the answer panel (isOpen prop). It lacks aria-expanded, preventing assistive technology users from knowing if the FAQ item is currently open or closed.
